original_hypothesis.title: No User Self-Cancel Mechanism Creates Permanent Fund Lock If Manager Becomes Unavailable
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: cancelDeposit and cancelWithdrawal are both onlyManager. If the manager becomes unresponsive (key loss, compromise, dispute), users have no mechanism to recover queued assets. The owner can change the manager via setManager, but even a new manager must actively process cancellations. If both owner and manager are unavailable, user funds are permanently locked with no timeout-based escape hatch.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the trust model states managers are trusted, the absence of a user self-cancel mechanism creates a systemic risk where a single point of failure (manager availability) can lock arbitrary amounts of user funds. This is a protocol design gap that becomes critical if the manager key is lost or if there's a governance dispute.

proof_of_signal.pattern: cancelDeposit is onlyManager with no user-callable alternative or timeout-based self-cancel

preconditions: Manager becomes unavailable (key loss, compromise, dispute)
Owner is also unavailable or unable to set a new responsive manager
Users have pending deposit or withdrawal requests

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No timeout-based self-cancel: users must rely entirely on manager availability
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Manager unavailability locks all pending user funds indefinitely
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No profit extraction; pure fund-locking DoS for all queued users

economic_questions: What is the expected manager uptime and key management setup?
Is there a governance process to replace an unavailable manager?
What is the maximum TVL that could be locked in pending requests?
validation_requirements: Verify no alternative path exists for users to recover queued assets
Assess whether owner can fully recover the situation via setManager
Evaluate whether a time-delayed self-cancel mechanism is feasible

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users with pending deposits or withdrawals
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of queued deposit or burned withdrawal shares locked
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total pending deposits + value of shares in pending withdrawals

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Manager key management setup and governance procedures
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify all code paths require manager for fund recovery

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
